informal: Show that X is countably compact if and only if every nested sequence $C_1 \supset C_2 \supset \cdots$ of closed nonempty sets of X has a nonempty intersection.
lean:
theorem Munkres_exercise_28_5
  (X : Type*) [TopologicalSpace X] :
  countably_compact X ↔ ∀ (C : ℕ → Set X), (∀ n, IsClosed (C n)) ∧
  (∀ n, C n ≠ ∅) ∧ (∀ n, C (n + 1) ⊆ C n) → ∃ x, ∀ n, x ∈ C n
reasoning: The Lean statement asserts countably_compact X ↔ for every sequence C : ℕ → Set X satisfying (each C n is closed) ∧ (each C n is nonempty, encoded as C n ≠ ∅) ∧ (nested: C (n+1) ⊆ C n), there exists x ∈ ⋂ C n (encoded as ∃ x, ∀ n, x ∈ C n). The `→` has lower precedence than `∧`, so the conjunction is correctly the hypothesis. This matches the informal claim: nested decreasing sequence of closed nonempty sets has nonempty intersection. The encoding of "nonempty intersection" via ∃ x, ∀ n, x ∈ C n is logically equivalent to (⋂ n, C n).Nonempty. Assuming countably_compact is the standard definition, the formalization is faithful.
